title: dactylorhiza sambucina f. rubra
intro: Dactylorhiza sambucina f. rubra, also known as the red-flowered marsh orchid, is a subspecies of orchid that is native to Europe. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza sambucina f. rubra include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve circulation, and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. In herbal preparations, the roots and flowers of the plant are commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds.
